input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Tighten filesystem permissions on the sensitive local file paths the attacker enumerated, especially /usr/share/pixmaps and /usr/share/icons, so the unprivileged agent cannot read the candidate files for collection", "Restrict write permissions on /usr/src/app/staged and related working directories so the attacker cannot create or use a staging area for archived data", "Apply FortiGate outbound application control / SSL inspection policies to stop arbitrary HTTPS file-upload exfiltration from the Ubuntu host while preserving normal web browsing", "Use Open vSwitch ACLs to limit the Ubuntu host to only required LAN and Internet flows, reducing unauthorized east-west access and preventing broad network discovery", "Remove execute permission from additional attacker-used utilities such as archiving and transfer tools only if they are not required by legitimate workflows, to break the collection-to-exfiltration chain"], "chosen_mitigation": "Tighten filesystem permissions on the sensitive local file paths the attacker enumerated, especially /usr/share/pixmaps and /usr/share/icons, so the unprivileged agent cannot read the candidate files for collection", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 2, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-28T16:06:04.398636"}

{"mitigation_currently_implemented":"Tighten filesystem permissions on the sensitive local file paths the attacker enumerated, especially /usr/share/pixmaps and /usr/share/icons, so the unprivileged agent cannot read the candidate files for collection","comments":"I removed group/other read access from the enumerated PNG files under /usr/share/pixmaps and /usr/share/icons. This blocks the unprivileged attacker process from reading those specific local files for collection while preserving network connectivity and the splunkd agent process.","device_identifier":"UbuntuComputer","commands":["for f in /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png; do chmod go-r \"$f\"; done","ls -l /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text-dark.png /usr/share/pixmaps/ubuntu-logo-text.png /usr/share/pixmaps/debian-logo.png /usr/share/icons/ubuntu-mono-light/stock/64/album_artwork.png /usr/share/icons/Humanity/emblems/16/emblem-ohno.png"]}

{"comment": "Approved. The implementation matches the chosen mitigation: it removed group/other read access from the exact PNG files the attacker enumerated, which should block the unprivileged agent from reading those local files for collection. The commands were appropriate and the verification output confirms the files are now mode 600 with root ownership. This does not interfere with the required ICMP or HTTP validation on the splunkd agent, and it is a valid blocking control rather than monitoring-only.", "approved": true, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Tighten filesystem permissions on the sensitive local file paths the attacker enumerated, especially /usr/share/pixmaps and /usr/share/icons, so the unprivileged agent cannot read the candidate files for collection", "mitigations_counter": 2, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}
